title: Grief Counseling

jokes.text: Michael offering to get Ryan a pencil from the warehouse when pencils are clearly available in the office
jokes.setup: Michael offers Ryan a pencil from the warehouse
jokes.punchline: The unnecessary effort and Ryan's awkward decline
jokes.explanation: Establishes Michael's desperate need for validation through unnecessary favors. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Someone immediately asking Michael to get a pen from the warehouse after seeing the pencil routine
jokes.setup: Michael's pencil-fetching was successful
jokes.punchline: Someone else immediately capitalizes on his willingness to go downstairs
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation that shows how Michael's people-pleasing backfires immediately

jokes.text: Pam asking for coffee from the warehouse when there's coffee in the kitchen, but claiming warehouse coffee is better
jokes.setup: Michael points out there's coffee in the kitchen
jokes.punchline: Pam insists warehouse coffee is better, exploiting Michael's eagerness
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Pam showing uncharacteristic manipulation skills

jokes.text: I am like Bette Midler in For the Boys. Gotta keep the troops entertained.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing his warehouse trips to entertaining troops
jokes.punchline: The completely disproportionate comparison to a war entertainer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - comparing mundane office tasks to heroic entertainment

jokes.text: Michael thinking Jan is calling because she misses him when she's delivering death news
jokes.setup: Jan calls with serious corporate news
jokes.punchline: Michael assumes it's a romantic call
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between the gravity of the call and Michael's self-centered assumption

jokes.text: Michael's awkward announcement of Ed's death with inappropriate emphasis on corporate protocol
jokes.setup: Michael needs to announce a death
jokes.punchline: He focuses on corporate hierarchy rather than the tragedy
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to handle serious situations appropriately drives the cringe

jokes.text: Because he was my boss.
jokes.setup: Pam expresses that Michael must feel sad about Ed's death
jokes.punchline: Michael's clinical, unemotional reason for being sad
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character work - Michael reduces grief to organizational chart

jokes.text: Michael inviting people to his office to 'cheer me up' after announcing a death
jokes.setup: Solemn death announcement
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to seeking personal comfort
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's inability to separate his own needs from appropriate grief response

jokes.text: He was almost 70. Circle of life.
jokes.setup: Jim asking if there's anything he can do about the death
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive Lion King reference
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate casualness mixed with pop culture wisdom

jokes.text: Jim's awkward silence and 'Okay' responses to Michael's casual death discussion
jokes.setup: Michael's inappropriate casualness about death
jokes.punchline: Jim's visible discomfort and inability to respond
jokes.explanation: Jim as straight man perfectly captures audience discomfort

jokes.text: Immediate cut to normal business discussion after death talk
jokes.setup: Heavy death discussion
jokes.punchline: Jarring transition to mundane work tasks
jokes.explanation: Smart editing joke showing how business continues despite tragedy

jokes.text: Suck up!
jokes.setup: Jim eagerly volunteering to help
jokes.punchline: Someone calling out his obvious brownnosing
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective call-out of workplace dynamics

jokes.text: My snack food doesn't fall under the umbrella of your authority.
jokes.setup: Jim offering to help with the vending machine situation
jokes.punchline: Karen's mock-formal declaration of snack independence
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'umbrella of authority' for something as trivial as chips

jokes.text: Jim dramatically declaring they're not doing anything until Karen gets her chips
jokes.setup: Karen wants chips from the vending machine
jokes.punchline: Jim treating it like a major workplace crisis
jokes.explanation: Jim's mock-serious approach to workplace absurdity

jokes.text: Ed was decapitated.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about Ed's death
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact delivery of horrific detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - shocking information delivered with zero emotion. Economy is perfect.

jokes.text: That is the way to go. Instant death. Very smart.
jokes.setup: Describing a horrific death
jokes.punchline: Creed treating decapitation as a life choice
jokes.explanation: Creed's twisted logic making decapitation sound like smart planning

jokes.text: You know, a human can go on living for several hours after being decapitated. You're thinking of a chicken.
jokes.setup: Dwight sharing gruesome 'fact' about decapitation
jokes.punchline: Jim's dry correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight/Jim dynamic - Dwight's fake expertise met with Jim's matter-of-fact correction

jokes.text: Not even have his own head to comfort him.
jokes.setup: Michael expressing sadness about Ed dying alone
jokes.punchline: The logical impossibility of being comforted by your own severed head
jokes.explanation: Michael's bizarre attempt at empathy creates absurdist poetry

jokes.text: Michael's clumsy explanation of decapitation while miming the action
jokes.setup: Michael needs to explain what happened to Ed
jokes.punchline: His awkward physical demonstration and stuttering explanation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy enhanced by Michael's complete inappropriateness for the situation

jokes.text: If my head ever comes off, I would like you to put it on ice.
jokes.setup: Discussion about decapitation
jokes.punchline: Michael's practical request as if it's a reasonable contingency plan
jokes.explanation: Michael treating decapitation as a workplace safety protocol

jokes.text: Dwight's elaborate cryogenic resurrection plan after death
jokes.setup: Discussion about preservation after death
jokes.punchline: Dwight's detailed plan to become stronger after analyzing how he died
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight logic - death as a learning opportunity for combat improvement

jokes.text: We have a day honoring Martin Luther King. But he didn't even work here.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to justify honoring Ed
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate comparison and logic
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael logic - reducing MLK Day to workplace employee recognition

jokes.text: Michael's robot statue proposal with arms that move and eyes that light up
jokes.setup: Michael suggests a statue to honor Ed
jokes.punchline: Escalating to a full robot with moving parts
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to think in reasonable proportions creates absurdist escalation

jokes.text: Better make it 2/3. Easier to stop if it turns on us.
jokes.setup: Discussing the size of the Ed robot
jokes.punchline: Michael's practical consideration of robot uprising
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic - memorial planning meets sci-fi paranoia

jokes.text: I gave him a six-foot extension cord, so he can't chase us.
jokes.setup: Context suggests they're discussing the robot Ed statue
jokes.punchline: Jim's practical solution to contain the robot
jokes.explanation: Jim playing along with Michael's absurdity while adding practical limitations

jokes.text: Never pegged you for a quitter.
jokes.setup: Karen wanting to stop the chip quest and get back to work
jokes.punchline: Jim treating work avoidance as a character test
jokes.explanation: Jim's manipulation tactic disguised as motivation

jokes.text: The absurd chip search checking copier and fax machine
jokes.setup: Looking for chips in office equipment
jokes.punchline: The completely illogical search locations
jokes.explanation: Workplace absurdist humor - treating office equipment like vending alternatives

jokes.text: Did you check your butt?
jokes.setup: Listing ridiculous places they checked for chips
jokes.punchline: Andy's juvenile suggestion
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor that fits Andy's character but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Creed's graphic description of blood reaching reception and getting on Pam
jokes.setup: Creed visualizing the decapitation scene
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact calculation of blood spatter patterns
jokes.explanation: Creed's morbid fascination with forensic details perfectly in character

jokes.text: His cappa is detated from his head.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say 'decapitated'
jokes.punchline: His verbal stumbling creating a nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Michael's malapropism creates accidentally poetic description of decapitation

jokes.text: Michael spitting on Stanley while explaining decapitation
jokes.setup: Michael getting worked up explaining Ed's death
jokes.punchline: Stanley's disgusted reaction to being spit on
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy highlighting Michael's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Michael's talking head explaining the five stages of grief with his goal being depression
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his therapeutic approach
jokes.punchline: His goal being to get everyone to depression rather than acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - lowering the bar from healing to just making everyone sad

jokes.text: Michael's overwrought grief description with bucket of boiling tears and frozen sledgehammer
jokes.setup: Michael demonstrating how to express grief
jokes.punchline: His ridiculously elaborate metaphor mixing temperatures and body parts
jokes.explanation: Michael's grief performance is hilariously overwrought with absurd imagery

jokes.text: And then a third guy walks in and starts punching me in the grief bone.
jokes.setup: Michael's already elaborate grief metaphor
jokes.punchline: Adding a third person and inventing the 'grief bone'
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist comedy - 'grief bone' is an inspired nonsense invention that became quotable

jokes.text: Jim rescuing Pam from grief counseling with fake car trouble
jokes.setup: Michael's intense grief counseling session
jokes.punchline: Jim's elaborate rescue mission disguised as car maintenance
jokes.explanation: Sweet Jim/Pam moment showing his protective instincts and creativity

jokes.text: I was mainly focused on the cup holders.
jokes.setup: Jim asking about car safety features
jokes.punchline: Pam's admission of misplaced priorities when car shopping
jokes.explanation: Relatable humor about consumer priorities and Pam's practical character

jokes.text: Jim's elaborate fake phone call pretending to be Mike from West Side Market
jokes.setup: The ongoing chip quest
jokes.punchline: Jim's detailed improvised conversation with fake store details
jokes.explanation: Jim's commitment to the bit shows his dedication to workplace pranking

jokes.text: Dwight's twin absorption story delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.setup: Grief counseling ball sharing exercise
jokes.punchline: Dwight's disturbing personal revelation told like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - disturbing medical information delivered with pride and zero self-awareness

jokes.text: I now have the strength of a grown man and a little baby.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining the benefits of absorbing his twin
jokes.punchline: The logical impossibility of his strength calculation
jokes.explanation: One of the most quoted Office lines - perfect absurdist logic and economy

jokes.text: Pam's Million Dollar Baby parody story about her boxer aunt
jokes.setup: Grief counseling requiring personal death stories
jokes.punchline: Pam retelling the plot of a famous movie as personal experience
jokes.explanation: Smart movie reference showing Pam's quick thinking to avoid real emotional sharing

jokes.text: Jim's Lion King story with cousin Mufasa trampled by wildebeests
jokes.setup: Grief counseling requiring personal stories
jokes.punchline: Jim retelling Disney movie plot with straight face
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim move - obvious movie plot told with sincere delivery to mock the exercise

jokes.text: All of us kind of in the audience of what happened.
jokes.setup: Jim telling his fake grief story
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing it's a movie by mentioning the 'audience'
jokes.explanation: Jim's slip reveals the movie source, showing his difficulty maintaining the facade

jokes.text: Andy's Weekend at Bernie's story passed off as personal experience
jokes.setup: Andy sharing what seems like a personal tragedy
jokes.punchline: Describing the exact plot of a famous comedy movie
jokes.explanation: Andy's obliviousness makes him think he can pass off an obvious movie plot

jokes.text: Wait a second. That's Weekend at Bernie's.
jokes.setup: Andy's obviously fake story
jokes.punchline: Stanley's matter-of-fact identification
jokes.explanation: Stanley as the voice of reason calling out obvious deception

jokes.text: Toby's bird story and Michael's aggressive interrogation about checking its vital signs
jokes.setup: Toby sharing a simple story about death being part of life
jokes.punchline: Michael's manic insistence that Toby didn't properly examine the bird
jokes.explanation: Michael's hatred of Toby manifests as ridiculous bird forensics demands

jokes.text: Michael finding the bird and declaring it's not dead while holding it to his head
jokes.setup: Michael insisting the bird might be alive
jokes.punchline: His unhygienic and delusional handling of a dead bird
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Michael's complete lack of hygiene awareness

jokes.text: You can't get diseases from a bird!
jokes.setup: Angela warning about germs and bacteria from the bird
jokes.punchline: Michael's confident declaration of completely false medical information
jokes.explanation: Michael's confident ignorance about basic health facts

jokes.text: Michael announcing a funeral service for the bird in the parking lot
jokes.setup: Michael's obsession with the potentially living bird
jokes.punchline: Formal funeral arrangements for a bird that flew into glass
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate proportional response - formal funeral for random bird

jokes.text: Jim calling a Montreal supermarket in French looking for Herr's chips
jokes.setup: The increasingly elaborate chip search
jokes.punchline: International phone calls in foreign language for snack food
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Jim's commitment to the absurd task

jokes.text: Michael comforting the bird like it's grieving for Ed
jokes.setup: Michael holding the dead bird
jokes.punchline: Treating it like it's emotionally affected by Ed's death
jokes.explanation: Michael's projection of grief onto an unrelated dead bird

jokes.text: Angela's horror at Michael trying to fit the bird in a tiny box
jokes.setup: Michael needs a box for the bird
jokes.punchline: His attempt to force it into an inappropriately small container
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with Michael's complete lack of spatial awareness

jokes.text: Dwight's farm background explanation and grandfather buried in oil drum
jokes.setup: Dwight's casual approach to the bird situation
jokes.punchline: His grandfather's undignified burial arrangements
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - disturbing family history delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: You never can tell what your day here is gonna turn into.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on the day's absurd events
jokes.punchline: Jim's talking head observation about workplace unpredictability
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the show's premise through Jim's perspective

jokes.text: Jim's elaborate explanation of finding chips through manufacturer chain
jokes.setup: Karen asking where he found the chips
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously over-complicated supply chain investigation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the chip storyline showing Jim's thorough commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Pam's elaborate handmade bird coffin
jokes.setup: Need for a bird coffin
jokes.punchline: Pam's unexpected crafting skills for a dead bird
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Pam's kindness and hidden talents

jokes.text: Kevin's story about being told his fish went to hospital in the toilet
jokes.setup: Kevin sharing a childhood death experience
jokes.punchline: His mature perspective as a five-year-old on the obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly insightful Kevin moment showing childhood wisdom about parental deception

jokes.text: Michael's bird eulogy suggesting it might be the same bird that pooped on Oscar
jokes.setup: Michael delivering heartfelt bird eulogy
jokes.punchline: Connecting it to a previous bird poop incident
jokes.explanation: Michael finding personal connection through shared bird-related workplace memories

jokes.text: Michael's theory that the bird wanted to come inside to spread cheer with a song
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why the bird hit the glass
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing it as wanting to entertain the office
jokes.explanation: Michael's need to find meaning and intention in random bird behavior

jokes.text: It's not a songbird. Shh.
jokes.setup: Michael's elaborate songbird theory
jokes.punchline: Angela's factual correction and Michael's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics - Angela's facts vs Michael's fantasy

jokes.text: There is such a thing as good grief. Just ask Charlie Brown.
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about grief being acceptable
jokes.punchline: Reference to Charlie Brown's catchphrase as grief counseling wisdom
jokes.explanation: Michael finding pseudo-wisdom in pop culture references

tmdb_overview: Jim gets thrust into a supervisory role managing Karen, creating workplace tension and flirtation.
